summary: I facilitated a real student software team (peer review tool) with measurable delivery improvement, while comparing my approach to published agile transformations at ING, Spotify, and UK GDS.

start_here.label: Read the case study
start_here.description: What the team struggled with, what I changed, and outcomes in plain language.

start_here.label: Explore the analytics dashboard
start_here.description: KPIs, sprint throughput, impediment log, retros, timeline, and GitHub delivery activity.

start_here.label: Verify the delivery codebase
start_here.description: The product the team shipped — separate repo with public commits.

requirements.my_evidence: Introduced 15-minute daily, capped planning at 90 minutes, biweekly retro with exactly one action item.
Shared impediment log with owners; CI blocker assigned in daily and closed in 2 days.
Sprint goals, point tracking, and sprint summaries before course demos.
Team DoD checklist (review, tests, PR description, no red CI) enforced after retro action.
requirements.metric: Documented in working agreement + retro sprint 1

requirements.my_evidence: Acceptance criteria on backlog items before planning; product owner feedback captured in quotes.
Compared my facilitation to ING, Spotify, and GDS public agile stories — same levers, different scale.
requirements.metric: 4 case studies + compare matrix in dashboard
